political.s: Founder William Bolthouse made $100,000 donation to Proposition 8 (2008) and supported right-wing causes including Christian fundamentalist organizations through the separate Bolthouse Foundation. Company claims independence from Foundation since 2005 sale to Madison Dearborn Partners, though governance connections remain disputed.

charity.s: Bolthouse Farms donated over $4 million during COVID-19 pandemic to Feeding America and local food banks, including product donations and employee matching programs. Company focuses charitable giving on healthier eating and nutrition, community support, and employee well-being.
charity.focus: Healthier eating, nutrition access, food security, community support

environment.s: Company established ESG Hub in 2022 to strengthen environmental commitments and received Best Sustainable Packaging Award. Implementing regenerative agriculture practices including autonomous weeding technology and precision farming to reduce chemical use.

labor.s: Company offers safety protocols, on-the-job training, and employee benefits including health/wellness programs. However, Indeed reviews show mixed satisfaction, with some reports of hard labor, inconsistent pay, and concerns about restructuring following Butterfly Equity ownership in 2019.

dei.s: Company policy states non-discrimination on sexual orientation and provides benefits to same-sex couples. However, founder's historical donations to anti-gay initiatives and Foundation's evangelical focus create historical DEI concerns, though current leadership appears supportive.

animals.s: Not applicable - produce and beverage company does not conduct animal testing.

guns.s: Not applicable - company operates in food and beverage sector with no connection to firearms.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
